activity: Pacific Rim National Park Reserve Entry
description: All visitors to Pacific Rim National Park Reserve (Long Beach, Broken Group Islands, West Coast Trail) must pay a Parks Canada entry fee or hold a Discovery Pass.
where: Parks Canada kiosks at park entrances or online

seasonalWindow.notes: The Discovery Pass covers all Parks Canada sites nationwide and is good value for multi-day or multi-park trips.

tips: The Discovery Pass pays for itself in 2 days at Pacific Rim.
Purchase online before arrival to avoid queues at entrance kiosks.
The pass also covers the West Coast Trail entry (permit fee is separate).

description: The West Coast Trail requires a hiking permit ($255 CAD + reservation fee) and a mandatory pre-trip orientation. Permits are limited and release in January for the May–September season.

seasonalWindow.notes: Permits release in January and sell out quickly. A limited number of walk-up permits are available at trailheads (Bamfield and Port Renfrew) — arrive before 6 AM for a chance.

tips: Book in January when reservations open — peak dates sell out within days.
The mandatory orientation session covers tides, wildlife, and trail conditions.
Plan 6–8 days to complete the 75 km trail comfortably.
Water taxi fees at trailheads are additional (~$100–150 CAD).
Solid backcountry experience is required — the trail involves ladders, cable cars, and tidal passages.

description: The only campground inside Pacific Rim's Long Beach unit. Clifftop sites above the Pacific. Reservations open in February and fill within hours for summer dates.

seasonalWindow.notes: Reservations open in February for the full season. Generator-free zone.

tips: Book on opening day in February for July/August dates.
Sites on the ocean side are the most coveted — select carefully.
The campground is a short walk from Long Beach surf breaks.
No generators allowed — bring a battery pack if needed.

description: Camping in Strathcona Provincial Park (Buttle Lake, Ralph River) requires reservations through the BC Parks system.
cost: $18–$35 CAD/night depending on campground and site type

seasonalWindow.notes: Reservations open 4 months ahead on a rolling basis via the Discover Camping system.

tips: Buttle Lake campground is the most popular — book 4 months ahead.
No day-use fee for the park itself — only camping requires payment.
Check bcparks.ca for current trail conditions before hiking.
